dependencies: Limited number of companies capable of supplying network infrastructure equipment
Single vendor for manufacture and supply of critical components such as chipsets used in devices
Suppliers located or rely on operations outside of the U.S.
risks: Disruption to global supply chain as a result of geopolitical factors
Disruption from natural disasters or potential impacts of global climate change
Performance issues or delays from key suppliers
Failure to provide equipment or service on a timely basis
International operations increase exposure to economic and geopolitical instability, fluctuations in exchange rates, and labor-related risks
Certain business practices in foreign countries may not align with U.S. laws and regulations

regulatory_risks: FCC regulation of wireless operations including construction, operation, acquisition and transfer of wireless communications systems
FCC spectrum license renewal challenges
Open access FCC regulations requiring licensees to allow customers to use devices and applications of their choice
State and local regulation of terms and conditions of wireless service
State reporting and consumer protection requirements
State laws addressing safety issues such as use of wireless handsets while driving
State universal service funding requirements
State taxation matters
State and local zoning and land use regulation for wireless tower and antenna facilities
Potential utility-style common carriage regulation of broadband services under Title II of the Communications Act
State net neutrality laws and executive orders
New York law requiring discounted broadband pricing for low income customers
State pricing or other rules related to broadband built with federal or state funding
FCC digital discrimination rules
State public utility commission regulation of telephone operations
State network reliability or service quality requirements for wireline and wireless voice services
Local, state, federal, and international privacy and data protection laws including California Consumer Privacy Act and Europe's General Data Protection Regulation
Potential laws and regulations on the use of artificial intelligence at local, state, federal and international levels
FCC regulations on emergency communications services and continuity of service during disasters
California Public Utilities Commission regulations on back-up power for communications facilities
FCC and other regulators increasing regulation of cybersecurity practices
FCC restrictions on use of equipment from certain companies deemed to cause national security risks
FCC regulation of intercarrier compensation and network access rates
Climate-related regulation including direct regulation of greenhouse gas emissions or carbon policies
Policy-driven changes in prices of fuel or energy
Increased taxes on fuel
Changes in tax laws or regulations or their interpretation
Challenges to tax positions resulting in additional tax expense or liabilities
